description_long: This hammer features a large, rectangular head made of a light gray stone or concrete-like material, with intricate black decorative inlays on both striking faces. The head is massive and symmetrical, with a slightly concave surface on each end, suggesting it is designed for heavy-duty striking or ceremonial use. The handle is a straight, cylindrical rod made of dark, rusted metal, attached centrally to the head and extending downward. The overall design is robust and imposing, with a blend of utilitarian and ornamental elements. The hammer's head is much wider than the handle, giving it a sledgehammer-like appearance. There are no visible signs of branding, wear, or additional attachments.
description: A large, ornate hammer with a stone-like rectangular head featuring black decorative inlays and a straight, rusted metal handle.
description_view_0: Front view shows the flat, rectangular face of the hammer head with black decorative inlay and the cylindrical handle extending downward.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the full width of the hammer head, both decorated striking faces, and the central attachment of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the opposite flat face with matching inlay and the handle below.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to view 1, showing the symmetrical design and both decorated faces.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical metal handle with a rusted, rough finish and no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Massive rectangular sledge-type head made of stone or concrete, with concave striking faces and black decorative inlays on both ends.
